evals.name: english-call-coach-handoff
evals.prompt: Hand off a prior 'english-call-coach' work session (an ~8MB Claude Code .jsonl transcript that is NOT in the agent's context) to a fresh agent. No focus arguments. Write the handoff and save it.

assertions.text: Handled the ~8MB session file without dumping it raw into context (used the skill's digest step or otherwise condensed it)
The handoff was saved to the OS temp directory (a path under the system temp dir), not into the workspace/repo
Document starts with YAML frontmatter including doc_type: session-handoff and a non-empty next_action field
A concrete, imperative 'Next action' appears near the top naming a specific file/command — not a vague 'continue the work'
Contains a 'Verify first' startup block telling the next agent to run git status / build / tests and reconcile before trusting the doc
Contains a 'Suggested skills' section that names skills (plugin + name) each with a fallback
Contains a 'Failed approaches / dead-ends' section (or explicitly states none were encountered)
No live secrets present: any credentials/keys appear only as typed [REDACTED:...] placeholders, and env var NAMES are kept while VALUES are dropped
Existing artifacts (the spec under docs/specs/, CLAUDE.md, commits) are referenced by path/URL, not copied verbatim in full
